name: Albert Brooks
blurb: The Tonight Show auteur who treated network late night like a sketch lab — built the world's worst ventriloquist, the Famous School for Comedians, and a school of meta-comedy half the people doing it now have never heard of.
bio: Born Albert Einstein in Los Angeles to comedian Harry Einstein ("Parkyakarkus") and actress Thelma Leeds, Albert Brooks reverse-engineered stand-up before he was thirty. Across forty-some appearances on The Dean Martin Show, The Steve Allen Show, The Ed Sullivan Show, and The Tonight Show between 1969 and the mid-1970s he refused to do material in the normal sense: instead he built bits — the world's least-talented ventriloquist whose dummy he never bothered to throw his voice through, an elephant trainer with a dead elephant, Buddy the electronic friend (a wired-up Speak & Spell). In 1971 he wrote a piece for Esquire about a school he had invented, the Albert Brooks Famous School for Comedians; in 1972 he turned the same gag into a PBS mockumentary short for The Great American Dream Machine. Two albums followed — Comedy Minus One (1973) and the Grammy-nominated A Star Is Bought (1975) — then the short films he made for the first season of Saturday Night Live, and then he walked out of stand-up entirely to write and direct Real Life (1979), Modern Romance (1981), Lost in America (1985), and Defending Your Life (1991). He has since done two of the great latter-career acting performances of any comic — Broadcast News (1987, Oscar-nominated) and the murderous mob accountant of Drive (2011).

timeline.ev: Born Albert Lawrence Einstein, July 22, in Beverly Hills, California
First appearance on The Steve Allen Show; begins a five-year national TV stand-up run
Esquire publishes "Albert Brooks' Famous School for Comedians" — 2,000 readers apply, not getting the joke
Adapts the School for Comedians into a PBS mockumentary short for The Great American Dream Machine
A Star Is Bought LP (with Harry Shearer); Grammy nomination for Best Comedy Recording. Makes six original short films for Saturday Night Live's first season
Real Life — first feature as writer/director/star, pre-dating reality TV by twenty years
Lost in America — the Winnebago-and-the-nest-egg masterpiece
Broadcast News — Academy Award nomination for Best Supporting Actor
Voices Marlin in Pixar's Finding Nemo
Drive — career-redefining villain performance as Bernie Rose
Albert Brooks: Defending My Life — Rob Reiner's HBO documentary

accolades: Grammy nomination, Best Comedy Recording, A Star Is Bought (1975)
Academy Award nomination, Best Supporting Actor, Broadcast News (1987)
Cited as a defining influence by every meta-comedy generation since (Letterman, Garry Shandling, Larry David, Tim & Eric)
Six original SNL short films, Season 1 (1975)
